steps.title: Read two points from the shown line
steps.content:
The graph shown is $y=f(x)-11$. Two clean points on that line are $(-1,-2)$ and $(0,-4)$. The slope is:
$$m=\frac{-4-(-2)}{0-(-1)}=-2$$

steps.title: Write the equation of the shown line
steps.content:
The y-intercept is $-4$, so the graphed line is:
$$y=-2x-4$$
Because the graphed line is $y=f(x)-11$, that means:
$$f(x)-11=-2x-4$$

steps.title: Solve for f(x)
steps.content:
Add $11$ to both sides:
$$f(x)=-2x+7$$
That matches choice B, so B is correct.
